questions.question: Explain the concept of Directory Services and its importance in enterprise networks.
keyPoints: Centralized storage of network resource information

questions.question: Describe the LDAP protocol and its operations.
keyPoints: LDAP stands for Lightweight Directory Access Protocol
Key operations: Bind, Search, Add, Modify, Delete, Compare

questions.question: What is Distinguished Name (DN) in LDAP? Explain its components.
keyPoints: DN is unique identifier for directory entry
RDNs concatenated from entry to root

questions.question: Explain the Directory Information Tree (DIT) structure.
keyPoints: Root at top, entries branch downward
Leaf entries contain actual user/resource data

questions.question: What is Active Directory? List its main components.
keyPoints: Microsoft's directory service for Windows domains

questions.question: Explain Single Sign-On (SSO) and its advantages.
keyPoints: SSO allows single authentication for multiple applications

questions.question: Describe the Kerberos authentication protocol used with directory services.
Explain the X.500 directory standard and how LDAP is derived from it. Discuss the advantages of LDAP over X.500 DAP.
keyPoints: X.500 is the OSI directory standard
Uses DAP (Directory Access Protocol) for client-server communication
LDAP derived from X.500 DAP but simplified
LDAP advantages: simpler protocol, works over TCP/IP, lower overhead, easier implementation
LDAP operates on Application Layer while X.500 spans full OSI model

questions.question: Discuss the security aspects of directory services including authentication mechanisms, access control, and encryption.
Design a directory structure for a university system with students, faculty, departments, and courses. Explain the hierarchy and attributes.
keyPoints: Each OU contains appropriate object classes
Use appropriate RDNs: uid for users, deptCode for departments
Consider nested groups for courses and committees

questions.question: Compare and contrast Active Directory, OpenLDAP, and other directory services. Discuss their features, advantages, and use cases.
keyPoints: Active Directory: Windows-centric, integrated with Microsoft ecosystem
Comparison: scalability, authentication protocols, management tools
Use cases: Enterprise Windows environments vs cross-platform Linux/Unix
